points: Data is any collection of facts, numbers, or observations used to convey information.
Organising raw data into a frequency distribution table using tally marks makes it readable and useful for analysis.
Frequency is the count of how many times each item appears in the data set.
A pictograph uses pictures or symbols to represent data frequencies, making it easy to interpret at a glance.
The scale or key must be specified to show how many units each symbol represents (e.g., 1 symbol = 5 objects).
Bar graphs use rectangular bars of uniform width and equal spacing to represent and compare data frequencies.
Choosing a suitable scale for the axis is important to ensure the graph accurately represents the data efficiently.
Horizontal bars are often used for lengths, while vertical bars (columns) are commonly used for heights.
Infographics are artistic data visualisations that use engaging imagery to communicate information quickly.
While aesthetics enhance appeal, care must be taken to ensure visuals do not mislead by distorting proportions or scales.
